pages.raw_text:
H. l.. Hughes said a few things
about the chamber of commerce.
You spend thousands of dollars to
advertise this city; you get work
ingmen here from the east to buy
real estate from your members at
q high price, or to pay you high
rents; your members who operate
stores here charge them an exorbi-
tant price for the necessities, and
yet you are not willing to pay
them living wage was the sub
stance of what he said regarding
the methods of that body.


He also said something about the
good salaries paid by the CONNER
cial body to it's officers, and show
ed that when the price of living is
taken into consideration, $3.50 in
Seattle or Tacoma is fully as much
as So in Spokane.


F. n. Chavez, business agent of
Federal union No. 12,222, and rep
resentatives of the other federal
unions and the transfers unions
also spoke for the workingman. Be
fore the close of the meeting the
cOuncilmen voted to ask Mayor
Pratt for recommendation as to
the raise in wages.
